decks.notes: The main focus of this deck is to control the opponent's board and hand through discard spells like Stupor and Chainer's Edict while using removal spells like Smother to deal with threats on the battlefield. This deck also aims to disrupt the opponent's graveyard strategies with cards like Planar Void and Withered Wretch. The win condition for this deck is through incremental advantage by using cards like Braids, Cabal Minion to constantly pressure the opponent's resources. The mana base is solid with a mix of basic Swamps and utility lands like Mishra's Factory and Wasteland.

The focus of this deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's game plan through a combination of targeted discard spells, removal, and hand disruption tools. By utilizing cards like Hypnotic Specter, Cursed Scroll, and The Rack, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and disrupt the opponent's hand and board state. Additionally, cards like Duress and Diabolic Edict help to remove key threats while Mishra's Factory and Wasteland provide additional utility and board presence. Overall, this mono black control deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver opponents through resource denial and efficient threats.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a swarm of aggressive Goblin creatures, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Piledriver to deal damage efficiently. The deck also includes cards like Gempalm Incinerator for removal and Goblin Matron for tutoring specific Goblins from the deck. Additionally, the inclusion of cards like Wasteland and Rishadan Port helps to disrupt the opponent's mana base, further solidifying the deck's aggressive playstyle and preventing the opponent from effectively countering the Goblin assault.

The deck aims to control the game by utilizing Oath of Druids to cheat out powerful creatures like Terravore early on, while also disrupting the opponent's game plan with cards like Swords to Plowshares and Mana Leak. It also has a suite of cards to manage the board and resources, such as Armageddon and Wasteland. The deck is designed to outvalue the opponent through efficient creature threats and card advantage provided by cards like Sylvan Library and Deep Analysis. It aims to establish and maintain board control while overwhelming the opponent with its threats.

The deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual and Cabal Therapy to efficiently play threats and disrupt the opponent's game plan, while also utilizing reanimation spells like Reanimate to bring back key creatures, the deck aims to quickly close out the game before the opponent can stabilize. With a combination of efficient creatures like Rotlung Reanimator and potent disruption like Duress, the deck seeks to apply consistent pressure and disrupt the opponent's strategy in order to secure victory.

The main focus of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's resources and slow down their game plan using a combination of land destruction, direct damage, and creature removal. By controlling the game with efficient removal spells and aggressive land destruction, the deck aims to outvalue the opponent and eventually close out the game with its low-curve creatures. The Mono Red Control archetype relies on managing the board state and resource denial to gain an advantage and ultimately secure victory through attrition.

The deck focuses on quickly filling the graveyard with powerful creatures like Akroma, Angel of Wrath and Phantom Nishoba using cards like Putrid Imp and Hapless Researcher. By using spells like Reanimate and Animate Dead, the deck can bring these creatures back into play for a low cost. The deck also includes disruption with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy to disrupt opponents' strategies. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a large, powerful board presence, allowing for quick victories. The deck utilizes the graveyard as a resource to quickly and efficiently bring out big threats.

The main focus of this deck is to quickly put pressure on the opponent by utilizing creatures with the Madness ability to play spells efficiently. By discarding cards like Arrogant Wurm and Basking Rootwalla, you can cast them for a reduced cost and overwhelm your opponent with aggressive plays. Utilizing cards like Circular Logic and Daze for disruption and control, this deck aims to outpace the opponent and maintain board presence with powerful creatures like Wild Mongrel and Roar of the Wurm. Additionally, cards like Merfolk Looter and Deep Analysis provide card advantage to keep the aggression going.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game through various removal spells and board wipes while using cycling cards to generate card advantage and maintain flexibility. By cycling through their deck, the player can consistently find the necessary answers to deal with threats and create advantage over their opponent. The deck aims to stall the game until they can overwhelm their opponent with a large number of tokens generated by cards like Decree of Justice while also utilizing powerful cycling synergies such as Astral Slide to control the board and gain incremental advantages.

The deck revolves around playing fast mana sources and interactive cards to set up for a game-winning combo of casting Balancing Act or Obliterate to reset the board and leave your opponent with few resources, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Orim's Chant. Burning Wish allows you to search for specific answers or win conditions in your sideboard. This deck aims to quickly assemble the necessary pieces for a powerful combo turn while managing resources effectively.

The main idea of the BW Control deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategy by utilizing hand disruption cards like Duress and Gerrard's Verdict, as well as removal spells like Swords to Plowshares and Vindicate. The deck also aims to generate card advantage through cards like Skeletal Scrying and Eternal Dragon, while being able to stabilize the board with sweepers like Wrath of God. Additionally, the deck has access to powerful finishers like Exalted Angel and Decree of Justice to close out the game. The mana base is designed to support the deck's color requirements.

Using a combination of efficient creature removal, hand disruption, and card draw, this deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan and apply pressure with aggressive creatures. It utilizes a mix of black and white spells to control the board and out-value the opponent, while also maintaining card advantage through effects like Phyrexian Arena. With a curve of powerful threats like Exalted Angel and Hypnotic Specter, the deck looks to close out games quickly before the opponent can stabilize.

The main focus of this deck is to rapidly deploy low-cost Goblin creatures and overwhelm the opponent with aggressive attacks. The deck utilizes cards like Goblin Lackey to quickly put threats on the battlefield, supported by goblin Lords like Goblin Warchief and Goblin King to boost their power. Removal cards like Gempalm Incinerator and Siege-Gang Commander help clear the way for attacks, while utility cards like Goblin Matron provide additional options. The mana base includes lands like Rishadan Port and Wasteland to disrupt the opponent's resources and keep the pressure on.

This Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on quickly building a board presence with inexpensive creatures and disruptive spells. By utilizing cards like Dark Ritual, Cabal Therapy, and Duress to disrupt the opponent's game plan, while deploying threats like Cabal Archon and Rotlung Reanimator, the deck aims to overwhelm the opponent before they can stabilize. With a low mana curve and the ability to apply pressure early on, this deck looks to close the game out swiftly and efficiently, while utilizing disruptive elements to maintain control of the game.

The deck revolves around using cards like Attunement and Intuition to fill the graveyard with enchantments, then casting Replenish to bring them all back onto the battlefield. The main combo involves using Opalescence to turn enchantments into creatures, then using Parallax Wave and Parallax Tide to exile all opposing threats. The deck also includes some control elements like Counterspell and Mana Leak, as well as removal in Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing. The mana base is streamlined to ensure consistency in casting the key combo pieces.

The goal of this combo Stasis deck is to lock down the opponent's resources and prevent them from untapping their lands using Stasis. By utilizing bounce spells like Chain of Vapor and countermagic like Arcane Denial, the deck aims to control the game and eventually win through card advantage and incremental damage from Black Vise. The deck relies on efficiently drawing through its library with cards like Gush and Impulse while also having the ability to protect the combo with counterspells like Counterspell and Thwart.
